text: The Cortex module in the EVA Tri-Brain architecture is responsible for high-level planning and reasoning. It runs on Claude Opus 4.7 for complex tasks and Gemini for alternative perspectives.
The Motor module handles code generation in the EVA agent. It uses Qwen running locally via Ollama, which is fast but has a smaller context window than the Cortex.
The Limbic module manages intent extraction and emotional context. It uses Typhoon, a Thai-tuned language model, because the primary user speaks Thai natively.
Memory in GKS is split into four layers: Atomic (the exact-lookup index), Vector (semantic search), Obsidian (graph and wikilink traversal), and Episodic (session history).
The Atomic layer reads atomic_index.jsonl and returns full notes by exact ID match. It never returns a close match — unknown IDs return null to prevent hallucination.
The Vector layer embeds text using bge-m3 (Ollama, 1024 dimensions) as the primary provider, falling back to text-embedding-3-small (OpenAI, 1536 dimensions) when Ollama is unreachable.
Cosine similarity in the Vector layer uses brute-force O(N*d) search. This is fine up to about 100,000 vectors; beyond that we migrate to HNSW or pgvector.
Bi-temporal retention lets a new fact supersede an older one by flipping valid_to on the predecessor and pointing superseded_by at the new doc. The old fact stays in the store for audit purposes.
The Obsidian adapter talks to the Local REST API plugin over HTTP. It supports semantic search via the plugin, backlink traversal, tag queries, and wikilink resolution.
Episodic memory is append-only. Session traces go into .brain/msp/projects/evaAI/session as JSONL; consolidated summaries go into the memory/ directory as markdown with YAML frontmatter.

The Consolidator runs at session end. It invokes an LLM (Anthropic Sonnet 4.6 by default) to produce a structured summary and candidate atomic proposals, then filters proposals through a deterministic Three-Gate scorer.

The inbound queue is the only authorized write path into anything destined for gks/. Artifacts live under .brain/msp/projects/evaAI/inbound and wait for human review before being promoted to the canonical index.
LoCoMo evaluates long-context memory with multi-session conversations. Target accuracy is 92% evidence@5 to match ByteRover 2.0 and EverOS.
LongMemEval tests temporal reasoning across sessions. Question types include single-session-user, single-session-assistant, single-session-preference, multi-session, and temporal-reasoning.
BEAM measures retrieval at 10-million-token scale. Targets: token savings above 90% (we surface at most 10% of corpus tokens per query) and p95 latency below 200 milliseconds.

The reranker runs as a second pass over the top-K first-pass hits. The default lexical BM25 scorer is zero-dep and always available; an HTTP backend lets you plug in BGE rerank, jina-reranker-v2, or any Hugging Face TEI server.
The temporal knowledge graph tracks relationships over time. Each edge has valid_from, valid_to, and recorded_at timestamps, plus an optional superseded_by pointer for auditable fact-replacement.

Session hooks: startSession loads the atomic index, verifies the vector manifest is compatible with the current embedder, pings Obsidian non-blockingly, and writes session.json. endSession flushes the trace and triggers consolidation when appropriate.

Write safety: the system never writes directly to gks/. All proposed atomic notes go through the inbound queue. Episodic files are append-only. Vector JSONL files may be rebuilt via scripts/msp/re-embed.ts but never overwritten destructively.

The re-embed script uses SHA-256 hashes in a manifest file to detect changed source files and skip re-embedding what hasn't changed. When the embedder model or dimension changes, it forces a full rebuild to prevent mixed-dimension stores.
